extraction.one_line_summary: DENNIS Rohan won the Stage 16 time trial, with YATES Simon extending his GC lead to 32 seconds over VALVERDE after QUINTANA and LÓPEZ disappointed.
tactical_patterns: YATES Simon built buffer before final mountain stages despite time trialing weakness, suggesting tactical evolution in race approach.
KELDERMAN Wilco's time trial performance moved him within 52 seconds of red jersey, making him live podium threat.
QUINTANA Nairo and LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel both cracked in same time trial, revealing shared altitude/fatigue accumulation pattern.

rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: controlled pacing strategy with mid-stage acceleration proves effective, now leaving race for Worlds preparation
rider_signals.ROSSKOPF Joey: positive: second place finish as domestique suggests BMC had residual team strength despite supporting leadership
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: gained 32-second GC buffer despite acknowledging time trialing weakness, mental preparation effective
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: positive: elevated to podium contention at 52 seconds after strong TT while GC rivals faded
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: dropped to fourth overall after disappointing time trial revealed accumulated fatigue from mountain efforts
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: negative: more disappointing than Quintana per commentary, suggesting youth/recovery deficit in third week
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: held second at 32 seconds but commentary suggests expectation gap versus performance
